reviews.titleZh: Chapter 1 — EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 1 — EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities
reviews.summaryZh: 这章先定义 EMS 系统怎么运作、paramedic 在系统中的定位，以及 profession、scope、medical direction 和 quality improvement 的基本框架。它本质上是在回答：你在 EMS 体系里是谁、你能做什么、你为什么要对自己的行为负责。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter defines how EMS systems function and where the paramedic fits within the system, including professionalism, scope, medical direction, and quality improvement. At its core, it answers: who you are in the EMS system, what you are allowed to do, and why you are accountable for your actions.
highlightsZh: 搞清 EMS system 的组成
理解 paramedic 的角色与责任
知道 scope、accountability 和 medical direction 的边界
highlightsEn: Understand EMS system structure
Define the paramedic role and responsibilities
Know the boundaries of scope, accountability, and medical direction
keyPointsZh: EMS 不是单一救护车，而是一个由 dispatch、现场救援、转运、接收系统、medical oversight 和 quality improvement 组成的完整系统。
paramedic / EMR 的角色不只是“把病人送到医院”，而是要在现场 assessment、initial management、decision-making 和 handoff 中承担专业责任。
scope of practice 决定你能做什么，但 scope 不是“你会什么”，而是“你被授权做什么”。
medical direction 是院前 care 的核心支持系统之一，说明你不是完全独立在做事，而是在一个受监管、受指导的专业体系中工作。
professionalism 不只是态度问题，它会影响病人信任、团队合作、法律风险和系统声誉。
quality improvement 说明 EMS 不是只做任务，而是要持续回顾系统表现、错误和改进空间。
keyPointsEn: EMS is not a single ambulance unit but a full system involving dispatch, scene care, transport, receiving systems, medical oversight, and quality improvement.
The paramedic/EMR role is not just to bring a patient to hospital, but to take professional responsibility for assessment, initial management, decision-making, and handoff.
Scope of practice defines what you are authorized to do; it is not simply what you personally know how to do.
Medical direction is a core support structure in prehospital care, showing that field providers work within a supervised and regulated professional system.
Professionalism is not just about attitude; it affects patient trust, teamwork, legal exposure, and system reputation.
Quality improvement means EMS is not just task performance—it is continuous review, learning, and system refinement.
mustKnowZh: 你必须分清 education / certification / licensure / registration 这些概念，它们都和“能不能执业、在什么边界下执业”有关。
你要理解 roles and responsibilities 不只是工作内容列表，而是 accountability 的来源。
medical direction、documentation、communication 和 system quality 其实从第一章就已经开始了，不是后面才出现的独立话题。
mustKnowEn: You must distinguish concepts such as education, certification, licensure, and registration, because all relate to whether and how you are allowed to practice.
Roles and responsibilities are not just a task list—they are a source of accountability.
Medical direction, documentation, communication, and system quality begin in Chapter 1 conceptually; they are not isolated topics that appear later by accident.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 scope of practice 理解成“我会做所以我能做”。实际上，scope 更接近“系统授权你做什么”。
常见误区 2：把 professionalism 当成软性内容。实际上它会直接影响病人安全、团队协作和法律责任。
常见误区 3：以为 quality improvement 是管理层的事。其实 frontline provider 也是 QI 的组成部分，因为你的记录、判断和执行都会进入系统回顾。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating scope of practice as ‘if I know how to do it, I can do it.’ In reality, scope is closer to what the system authorizes you to do.
Common confusion 2: viewing professionalism as soft content. In reality, it directly affects patient safety, teamwork, and legal responsibility.

Common confusion 3: thinking quality improvement belongs only to management. In fact, frontline providers are part of QI because their documentation, decisions, and performance feed back into system review.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic
reviews.summaryZh: 这章聚焦急救人员如何长期稳定工作，包括 stress、death and dying、自我保护和职业健康。它真正处理的是：如果你自己先垮掉，后面所有 clinical skill 都没有办法长期稳定发挥。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on how paramedics stay healthy and functional over time, including stress, death and dying, self-protection, and occupational health. At a deeper level, it asks: if you break down first, none of your clinical skills can be sustained over time.
highlightsZh: 识别职业压力来源
理解 burnout 和 self-protection
知道 emergency vehicle operations 也属于个人安全议题
highlightsEn: Recognize sources of occupational stress
Understand burnout and self-protection
Know that emergency vehicle operations are also personal-safety issues
keyPointsZh: paramedic well-being 不是附属话题，而是职业能力的一部分。
高压、睡眠不足、反复暴露于死亡和创伤，会长期影响判断、反应速度和情绪稳定。
well-being 包含心理、身体、职业和社交层面，不只是“有没有 burnout”。
death and dying 是 EMS 工作的常态风险源之一，不能等真正崩掉后才处理。
self-protection 既包括 PPE 和现场安全，也包括长期压力管理、休息和界限感。
emergency vehicle operations 不只是驾驶技术问题，它本质上也是安全与职业生存问题。
keyPointsEn: Paramedic well-being is not a side topic; it is part of professional capability.
High stress, sleep loss, and repeated exposure to death and trauma can affect judgment, reaction time, and emotional stability over time.
Well-being includes psychological, physical, occupational, and social dimensions—it is more than just burnout.
Death and dying are routine EMS stressors and should be addressed before they become overwhelming.
Self-protection includes PPE and scene safety, but also long-term stress management, rest, and personal boundaries.
Emergency vehicle operations are not just driving issues—they are also safety and professional-survival issues.
mustKnowZh: 你要把 stress management 当成 clinical reliability 的组成部分，而不是“软技能”。
面对死亡、哀伤和 repeated trauma exposure 时，早识别、早处理，比硬撑更专业。
个人安全不是自私，而是 EMS 能不能持续救人的前提。
mustKnowEn: You should treat stress management as part of clinical reliability, not as a ‘soft skill.’
With death, grief, and repeated trauma exposure, early recognition and early response are more professional than simply pushing through.
Personal safety is not selfish—it is a prerequisite for EMS to keep helping others.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 well-being 理解成心理健康附加题。其实它会直接影响现场判断和 patient safety。
常见误区 2：认为 burnout 只是“太累了”。实际上 burnout 常常会先表现为冷漠、错误增多、沟通下降和风险容忍度变化。
常见误区 3：觉得 emergency vehicle safety 只是驾驶章节内容。其实它本身就是 paramedic survival 的一部分。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating well-being as an optional mental-health add-on. In reality, it directly affects field judgment and patient safety.
Common confusion 2: thinking burnout is just ‘feeling tired.’ In reality, burnout often shows up first as detachment, increased mistakes, worse communication, and changes in risk tolerance.
Common confusion 3: assuming emergency vehicle safety belongs only in a driving chapter. It is actually part of paramedic survival and sustained readiness.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章把 EMS 从事后反应拉向前端 prevention，强调 paramedic 也参与社区健康和 injury prevention。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter shifts EMS from purely reactive care toward prevention, showing that paramedics also contribute to community health and injury prevention.
highlightsZh: 理解 prevention 的公共健康价值
认识 EMS 不只是 transport
把社区教育也看成 EMS 角色的一部分
highlightsEn: Understand the public-health value of prevention
Recognize EMS is more than transport
See community education as part of the EMS role

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲法律责任、过失、病患关系、documentation 和 reportable cases，帮助建立 legal boundary。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers legal accountability, negligence, the patient relationship, documentation, and reportable cases to establish legal boundaries.
highlightsZh: 搞清 negligence 和 accountability
知道 documentation 的法律价值
理解现场责任与 reportable cases
highlightsEn: Clarify negligence and accountability
Know the legal value of documentation
Understand scene responsibilities and reportable cases

reviews.summaryZh: 这章处理 clinical grey zone，重点在 patient autonomy、resuscitation 决策、integrity 和专业伦理框架。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter deals with clinical gray zones, focusing on patient autonomy, resuscitation decisions, integrity, and professional ethical frameworks.
highlightsZh: 学会区分 legal vs ethical
理解 patient autonomy
建立灰区判断框架
highlightsEn: Differentiate legal vs ethical issues
Understand patient autonomy
Build a framework for gray-zone decisions

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是后续所有 disease process 的底层科学，包括 cellular injury、acid-base、hypoperfusion、inflammation 和 stress response。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter provides the scientific foundation for later disease processes, including cellular injury, acid-base balance, hypoperfusion, inflammation, and stress responses.
highlightsZh: 理解病人为什么会这样
打稳后续 medical chapters 的底层逻辑
把 shock、sepsis、failure 都和 pathophysiology 连起来
highlightsEn: Understand why the patient looks this way
Build the foundation for later medical chapters
Link shock, sepsis, and failure back to pathophysiology

reviews.summaryZh: 这章建立药理底盘：药物命名、来源、分类、routes、pharmacokinetics、pharmacodynamics 和 medication safety。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter builds the pharmacology foundation: drug naming, sources, classification, routes, pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, and medication safety.
highlightsZh: 分清 pharmacokinetics vs pharmacodynamics
理解 routes 和 dosage safety
把后续所有 medication topics 打底
highlightsEn: Differentiate pharmacokinetics vs pharmacodynamics
Understand routes and dosage safety
Build the base for all later medication topics

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 8 — Vascular Access and Medication Administration
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 8 — Vascular Access and Medication Administration
reviews.summaryZh: 这章把药理从概念拉到执行，涵盖 IV/IO、fluids、电解质、dose/rate calculation 和 asepsis。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter turns pharmacology into execution, covering IV/IO access, fluids, electrolytes, dose/rate calculation, and asepsis.
highlightsZh: 理解 vascular access 的目的
掌握 dose / rate calculation 思路
把 medication administration 和 safety 绑定起来
highlightsEn: Understand the purpose of vascular access
Learn dose/rate calculation logic
Link medication administration with safety

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲不同年龄层的发育特点，提醒你不同年龄不是同一种病人的缩放版。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter reviews developmental stages across age groups and reminds you that patients at different ages are not simply scaled versions of one another.
highlightsZh: 把年龄层和 assessment 方式连起来
理解 growth / development 会影响沟通和 presentation
为 pediatrics / geriatrics 打底
highlightsEn: Link age stage to assessment approach
Understand how development affects communication and presentation
Build a foundation for pediatrics and geriatrics

reviews.summaryZh: 这章重点在 rapport、interview technique、nonverbal cues 和特殊沟通场景，帮助你真正拿到有用信息。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on rapport, interview technique, nonverbal cues, and special communication situations so that you can gather useful information.
highlightsZh: 用开放式问题拿病史
重视非语言讯号
把 communication 当成 assessment 的一部分
highlightsEn: Use open-ended questions to build the history
Pay attention to nonverbal cues
Treat communication as part of assessment

reviews.summaryZh: 这章训练 evidence-based EMS 思维，涵盖 research design、quality improvement 和 patient safety。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter trains evidence-based EMS thinking and covers research design, quality improvement, and patient safety.
highlightsZh: 理解 evidence-based practice
知道 quality improvement 的意义
把 patient safety 看成系统问题
highlightsEn: Understand evidence-based practice
Know the purpose of quality improvement
View patient safety as a systems issue

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 12 — Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 12 — Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada
reviews.summaryZh: 这章把视野从传统 transport 拉到 community paramedicine、expanded role 和 nontransport 争议。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter broadens the lens from traditional transport toward community paramedicine, expanded roles, and the nontransport debate.
highlightsZh: 认识 EMS role expansion
理解 community paramedicine
知道 nontransport 不是简单“不送”
highlightsEn: Recognize EMS role expansion
Understand community paramedicine
Know that nontransport is more than simply ‘not transporting’

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是生命维持入口，核心是 airway、oxygenation、ventilation 三者的关系，以及气道评估与管理。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter is a life-support entry point, focusing on the relationship among airway, oxygenation, and ventilation, along with airway assessment and management.
highlightsZh: 分清 airway / oxygenation / ventilation
知道 suction、adjunct、oxygen、BVM 的逻辑
把气道看成优先级问题
highlightsEn: Differentiate airway / oxygenation / ventilation
Understand the logic of suction, adjuncts, oxygen, and BVM
Treat the airway as a priority problem

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是 Chapter 13 的进阶版，从基础通气支持走向机械通气、ventilator management 和持续监测。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter is an advanced extension of Chapter 13, moving from basic ventilatory support into mechanical ventilation, ventilator management, and continuous monitoring.
highlightsZh: 理解机械通气不是“设好就完”
重视 patient response 与 monitoring
把通气支持和生理反应绑在一起看
highlightsEn: Understand that mechanical ventilation is not set-and-forget
Emphasize patient response and monitoring
Link ventilatory support with physiologic response

reviews.summaryZh: 这章训练你如何从病人、家属和旁观者那里获得真正有价值的临床线索。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter trains you to gather clinically meaningful information from the patient, family, and bystanders.
highlightsZh: 历史采集会决定后续查体方向
学会开放式提问和敏感话题处理
把 history 当成 clinical decision 的起点
highlightsEn: History guides the physical exam
Use open-ended questions and handle sensitive topics well
Treat history as the starting point for clinical decision-making

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲如何系统地看、摸、听、量，并把 findings 变成 clinical picture。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter teaches how to systematically inspect, palpate, auscultate, measure, and turn findings into a clinical picture.
highlightsZh: general impression 很关键
vital signs 要看趋势，不只看一次
physical exam 有局限，要和 history 合起来
highlightsEn: General impression is crucial
Vital signs should be viewed as trends, not one-time numbers
Physical exam has limits and must be integrated with history

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是 assessment 方法论主轴，把 scene assessment、initial assessment、focused exam、ongoing assessment 串成完整流程。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter is the methodological core of assessment, linking scene assessment, initial assessment, focused exam, and ongoing assessment into one process.
highlightsZh: assessment 是动态流程，不是死清单
scene 线索会改变节奏和优先级
ongoing assessment 很关键
highlightsEn: Assessment is dynamic, not a dead checklist
Scene clues change tempo and priorities
Ongoing assessment is essential

reviews.summaryZh: 这章把知识变成判断，重点在 critical thinking、priority setting 和从 theory 到 street application。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter turns knowledge into judgment, focusing on critical thinking, priority setting, and moving from theory to field application.
highlightsZh: 会做不等于会判断
最可能诊断不一定等于下一步最重要行动
protocol 要和 patient context 一起看
highlightsEn: Knowing steps is not the same as making judgments
The most likely diagnosis is not always the most important next action
Protocols must be read together with patient context

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调正确传达、交班和记录，让 clinical work 能被后续团队真正接住。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes communication, handoff, and documentation so that later teams can truly continue the clinical work.
highlightsZh: handoff 不只是说了什么，还包括先说什么
documentation 是 patient care continuity 的一部分
radio communication 会影响资源和交接
highlightsEn: Handoff is about what is said and what is said first
Documentation is part of continuity of care
Radio communication affects resources and transitions

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 20 — Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 20 — Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury
reviews.summaryZh: 这章训练你从机制和能量传递去推理潜在损伤，并理解 trauma system 的资源匹配逻辑。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter trains you to infer likely injury from mechanism and energy transfer while understanding trauma-system resource matching.
highlightsZh: MOI 是推理工具，不只是背景资料
高能量机制要警惕隐藏伤
不是最近医院最好，而是合适层级最好
highlightsEn: MOI is a reasoning tool, not just background info
High-energy mechanisms raise concern for hidden injury
The best destination is the right level of care, not simply the closest hospital

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是 trauma 里最要命的一条主线，重点在出血、灌注失败、休克和早期识别。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter is one of the most lethal trauma pathways, focusing on bleeding, perfusion failure, shock, and early recognition.
highlightsZh: 别等到晚期低血压才想到 shock
compensated shock 容易被低估
骨盆创伤常伴隐匿性失血
highlightsEn: Do not wait for late hypotension to think about shock
Compensated shock is easy to underestimate
Pelvic trauma often hides major blood loss

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲开放/闭合软组织伤、挤压伤和爆炸伤，强调外表不一定反映真正严重度。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers open and closed soft-tissue injury, crush injury, and blast injury, emphasizing that appearance may underestimate true severity.
highlightsZh: soft tissue injury 不只是皮肤问题
爆炸伤是多机制伤
处理重点是控制出血与保护组织
highlightsEn: Soft-tissue injury is more than a skin problem
Blast injury is a multi-mechanism injury
Management focuses on hemorrhage control and tissue protection

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调烧伤不是只看皮肤，而是全身性生理问题，并涉及评估、处理和 burn center 思维。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes that burns are not just skin injuries but systemic physiologic problems, involving assessment, management, and burn-center thinking.
highlightsZh: 面积和深度都重要
位置和吸入伤会改变风险
烧伤也可能引起液体与循环问题
highlightsEn: Both size and depth matter
Location and inhalation injury change risk
Burns can also create fluid and circulatory problems

reviews.summaryZh: 这章把 face、airway、brain injury 和 neuro risk 串起来，是 trauma 里高优先级章节。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter links facial trauma, airway concerns, brain injury, and neurologic risk, making it a high-priority trauma chapter.
highlightsZh: face injury 可能很快变成 airway problem
head injury assessment 要盯 neurologic deterioration
别只看外观，深层风险更重要
highlightsEn: Face injury can quickly become an airway problem
Head-injury assessment must watch for neurologic deterioration
Do not focus only on appearance; deeper risk matters more

reviews.summaryZh: 这章重点在识别潜在脊柱损伤并减少进一步神经损伤风险，而不是机械化固定。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on identifying possible spinal injury and reducing further neurologic harm rather than applying immobilization mechanically.
highlightsZh: 不要只看疼痛位置
机制、神经体征和风险背景都重要
保护神经功能是核心
highlightsEn: Do not focus only on pain location
Mechanism, neurologic signs, and risk context all matter
Protecting neurologic function is central

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是 trauma 和 airway/breathing 的连接点，重点在胸部损伤如何快速威胁 oxygenation、ventilation 和 circulation。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter links trauma with airway and breathing, focusing on how thoracic injuries rapidly threaten oxygenation, ventilation, and circulation.
highlightsZh: 胸部损伤优先级高
注意胸痛、呼吸困难、呼吸音变化
Thoracic trauma 常是 life threat
highlightsEn: Thoracic injury carries high priority
Watch for chest pain, dyspnea, and breath-sound changes
Thoracic trauma is often life-threatening

reviews.summaryZh: 这章训练识别“外表不明显但内部可能很糟”的腹部创伤，重点在 hidden bleeding 与 ongoing reassessment。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter trains recognition of abdominal trauma that may look subtle externally but be severe internally, especially hidden bleeding and the need for reassessment.
highlightsZh: 腹部创伤容易隐藏严重度
内出血和 shock 风险很关键
需要看趋势，不是只凭一次 palpation
highlightsEn: Abdominal trauma often hides severity
Internal bleeding and shock risk are key
Trend matters more than a single palpation

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲骨、关节、韧带和支撑结构损伤，重点在功能、固定、远端循环与神经状态。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers injuries to bones, joints, ligaments, and support structures, focusing on function, immobilization, distal circulation, and neurologic status.
highlightsZh: 不要只看痛不痛
固定是为了减少进一步损伤
distal pulse / sensation 很重要
highlightsEn: Do not judge only by pain
Immobilization helps reduce further injury
Distal pulse and sensation matter

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是 medical 高优先级主线之一，核心在氧合、通气、work of breathing 和 fatigue。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter is one of the highest-priority medical pathways, centered on oxygenation, ventilation, work of breathing, and fatigue.
highlightsZh: SpO2 不是全部
病人的 fatigue 和 work of breathing 很关键
呼吸病人会很快失代偿
highlightsEn: SpO2 is not the whole story
Fatigue and work of breathing are critical
Respiratory patients can decompensate quickly

reviews.summaryZh: 这章聚焦循环系统能否维持有效灌注，强调 chest pain、perfusion、skin signs 和 heart-lung thinking。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on whether the cardiovascular system can maintain effective perfusion, emphasizing chest pain, perfusion, skin signs, and heart-lung thinking.
highlightsZh: 胸痛不能只当疼痛看
skin signs 是 circulatory clue
心肺评估经常要一起看
highlightsEn: Do not treat chest pain as pain only
Skin signs are circulatory clues
Cardiac and respiratory assessment often belong together

reviews.summaryZh: 这章的重点是意识、运动、感觉和 baseline change，强调 time-sensitive neurologic assessment。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on consciousness, motor function, sensation, and baseline change, emphasizing time-sensitive neurologic assessment.
highlightsZh: 神经病人要看 baseline 差异
neurologic status 是动态的
时间敏感很重要
highlightsEn: Compare with baseline neurologic status
Neurologic status is dynamic
Time sensitivity matters

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲内分泌急症如何用非特异症状伪装自己，重点在历史、objective data 和整体表现。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter shows how endocrine emergencies can hide behind nonspecific symptoms, emphasizing history, objective data, and the overall picture.
highlightsZh: endocrine problem 常伪装成一般不舒服
血糖等客观数值很重要
baseline history 会改变判断
highlightsEn: Endocrine problems often masquerade as vague illness
Objective values like glucose matter
Baseline history changes interpretation

reviews.summaryZh: 这章虽然短，但高频高风险，核心在于分清轻度过敏和正在升级的 anaphylaxis。
reviews.summaryEn: Although brief, this is a high-frequency and high-risk chapter centered on distinguishing mild allergy from evolving anaphylaxis.
highlightsZh: 不要只看皮肤
声音改变和呼吸变化是 red flag
病情可能短时间内升级
highlightsEn: Do not look only at the skin
Voice change and breathing change are red flags
The condition can escalate quickly

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调 GI complaint 很常见，但背后病因差异很大，关键是看 severity 和隐藏风险。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes that GI complaints are common but can reflect very different levels of severity and hidden risk.
highlightsZh: 腹痛不是都一样
要警惕 dehydration、bleeding 和 surgical abdomen
general appearance 会改变风险判断
highlightsEn: Abdominal pain is not all the same
Watch for dehydration, bleeding, and surgical-abdomen risk
General appearance changes risk interpretation

reviews.summaryZh: 这章提醒你肾泌尿 complaint 不是单纯疼痛问题，还可能牵涉 infection、obstruction 和全身恶化。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter reminds you that renal and urologic complaints are not just pain problems but may involve infection, obstruction, and systemic deterioration.
highlightsZh: 既往史很关键
renal complaint 也可能系统性恶化
不要把它只当局部疼痛
highlightsEn: Past history is key
Renal complaints may become systemic
Do not reduce them to local pain only

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 36 — Toxicology: Substance Abuse and Poisoning
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 36 — Toxicology: Substance Abuse and Poisoning
reviews.summaryZh: 这章训练 tox thinking：presentation 很多样，往往要把 scene clues、history 和 patient appearance 拼起来。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter trains toxicology thinking: presentations vary widely, and you often need to combine scene clues, history, and patient appearance.
highlightsZh: toxicology 常像拼图
注意 airway / breathing / mentation 恶化
scene clues 很关键
highlightsEn: Toxicology is often puzzle-like
Watch for airway, breathing, and mentation deterioration
Scene clues are very important

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲血液系统如何通过氧输送、凝血和灌注问题影响全身。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers how the blood system affects the whole body through oxygen delivery, clotting, and perfusion problems.
highlightsZh: 血液问题常是全身性问题
结合症状和生命体征判断
要考虑 coagulation / oxygen delivery / perfusion
highlightsEn: Blood problems are often systemic problems
Integrate symptoms and vital signs
Think about coagulation, oxygen delivery, and perfusion

reviews.summaryZh: 这章聚焦环境本身就是病因的病人，重点在暴露史、体温、体液和持续暴露风险。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on patients in whom the environment itself is the cause, emphasizing exposure history, temperature, fluids, and ongoing exposure risk.
highlightsZh: scene 与 exposure history 很关键
先切断持续暴露
core temperature / hydration / perfusion 都相关
highlightsEn: Scene and exposure history are crucial
Stop ongoing exposure first
Core temperature, hydration, and perfusion are all related

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调 patient care 和 exposure control 必须并行，不能只顾病人也不能只顾传播。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes that patient care and exposure control must happen in parallel—you cannot focus on one and ignore the other.
highlightsZh: 病人照护与传播控制并行
PPE 和 scene management 很重要
不要只看 fever 一个症状
highlightsEn: Patient care and transmission control run in parallel
PPE and scene management matter
Do not judge everything by fever alone

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 OB/GYN 急症的双重风险：既有敏感沟通问题，也有出血、休克和 time-sensitive obstetric risk。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers the dual risk of OB/GYN emergencies: sensitive communication issues alongside bleeding, shock, and time-sensitive obstetric danger.
highlightsZh: 既要顾隐私，也要顾风险评估
OB emergency 要同时看母体和胎儿风险
妇科 complaint 也可能系统性恶化
highlightsEn: Balance privacy with risk assessment
OB emergencies require both maternal and fetal thinking
Gynecologic complaints can also become systemic emergencies

reviews.summaryZh: 这章聚焦 newborn 在出生后如何顺利完成适应，重点在呼吸、体温、色泽和整体适应状态。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on how the newborn successfully adapts after birth, emphasizing breathing, temperature, color, and overall transition status.
highlightsZh: 新生儿是特殊人群
temperature management 很关键
看 breathing / color / responsiveness
highlightsEn: Newborns are a distinct patient group
Temperature management is crucial
Watch breathing, color, and responsiveness

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调儿科不是小成人，重点在 appearance、caregiver input、代偿与快速恶化风险。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes that pediatric patients are not small adults, with focus on appearance, caregiver input, compensation, and rapid deterioration risk.
highlightsZh: 儿童不是缩小版成人
general appearance 高价值
孩子可能先代偿后快速恶化
highlightsEn: Children are not small adults
General appearance has high value
Children may compensate and then decline rapidly

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调老年病人的脆弱性、多病共存和 presentation 不典型，assessment 需要 baseline thinking。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes frailty, multimorbidity, and atypical presentation in older adults, requiring baseline-oriented assessment.
highlightsZh: geriatric patient 常 presentation 不典型
polypharmacy 很重要
baseline function 会改变判断
highlightsEn: Geriatric patients often present atypically
Polypharmacy matters
Baseline function changes interpretation

reviews.summaryZh: 这章的重点是面对特殊沟通、行为或功能障碍病人时，如何调整方法而不是放弃 assessment。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on adjusting your approach—rather than abandoning assessment—when caring for patients with communication, behavioral, or functional challenges.
highlightsZh: 沟通方式要跟着病人改
尊重、安全、耐心最重要
caregiver input 往往很有价值
highlightsEn: Adapt communication to the patient
Respect, safety, and patience matter most
Caregiver input is often highly valuable

reviews.summaryZh: 这章把 clinical care 放进真实任务环境，强调 scene、搬运、车辆、资源和操作安全。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter places clinical care inside the real operational environment, emphasizing scenes, movement, vehicles, resources, and operational safety.
highlightsZh: operations 直接影响 patient safety
lifting / moving 也是安全问题
EMS 不只是开车送人
highlightsEn: Operations directly affect patient safety
Lifting and moving are also safety issues
EMS is not just driving people to hospital

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 46 — Hazardous Materials, Incidents, and Response
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 46 — Hazardous Materials, Incidents, and Response
reviews.summaryZh: 这章的首要原则是 responder safety，强调识别 hazard、暴露方式、PPE 和去污。
reviews.summaryEn: The first principle of this chapter is responder safety, emphasizing hazard recognition, exposure route, PPE, and decontamination.
highlightsZh: 不要先把自己变病人
HazMat 不是普通 medical scene
decontamination 很关键
highlightsEn: Do not become another patient
HazMat scenes are not ordinary medical scenes
Decontamination is crucial

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲复杂事件中的组织结构、角色分配和统一控制，目的是把混乱变成可管理流程。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers organization, role assignment, and unified control in complex incidents, turning chaos into manageable process.
highlightsZh: 大场景需要 clear roles
incident management 提升效率与安全
不是 paperwork，而是 operational control
highlightsEn: Large scenes need clear roles
Incident management improves efficiency and safety
It is not paperwork—it is operational control

reviews.summaryZh: 这章训练 MCI 思维：从单病人最佳照护转向有限资源下的总体最大获益。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter trains MCI thinking: shifting from best care for one patient to the greatest overall benefit under limited resources.
highlightsZh: triage 是动态过程
rapid triage 很关键
MCI 思维和单病人场景不一样
highlightsEn: Triage is dynamic
Rapid triage is critical
MCI thinking differs from single-patient care

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调航空转运不是更快的救护车，而是一个空间、噪音、振动和资源都不同的环境。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes that air transport is not simply a faster ambulance—it is a different environment in terms of space, noise, vibration, and resources.
highlightsZh: air medical transport 有独立风险
patient packaging 更关键
前期准备和协调价值更高
highlightsEn: Air medical transport has its own risks
Patient packaging matters more
Preparation and coordination have even greater value

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 50 — Vehicle Extrication and Special Rescue
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 50 — Vehicle Extrication and Special Rescue
reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲特殊救援环境中如何安全接触、解救和转移病人，重点在 controlled rescue。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers how to safely access, disentangle, and move patients in special rescue settings, emphasizing controlled rescue.
highlightsZh: extrication 不是粗暴拖出来
scene safety 和 patient condition 要一起看
通常需要多团队协同
highlightsEn: Extrication is not rough dragging
Scene safety and patient condition must be considered together
It often requires multi-team coordination

reviews.summaryZh: 这章聚焦大型活动现场医疗，重点在 planning、resource layout、prevention 和 public-health style thinking。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter focuses on medical care at large events, emphasizing planning, resource layout, prevention, and public-health style thinking.
highlightsZh: mass gathering medicine 很依赖 planning
不是等病人来了再想
现场布局和 crowd risk 会改变照护模式
highlightsEn: Mass-gathering medicine depends heavily on planning
Do not wait for patients to arrive before thinking
Site layout and crowd risk change the care model

reviews.summaryZh: 这章处理恐袭场景中的持续威胁、scene instability、多机构响应和 responder safety。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter addresses ongoing threats, scene instability, multi-agency response, and responder safety in terrorism incidents.
highlightsZh: 先活下来再救人
注意二次威胁
situational awareness 和多机构协同都很关键
highlightsEn: Stay alive before trying to rescue
Watch for secondary threats
Situational awareness and multi-agency coordination are crucial

reviews.summaryZh: 这章是系统层级管理视角，强调 planning、logistics、resource management 和 recovery。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter takes a systems-level view, emphasizing planning, logistics, resource management, and recovery.
highlightsZh: 灾难管理不是临场 improvisation
logistics 很关键
recovery 也是灾难管理的一部分
highlightsEn: Disaster management is more than improvisation
Logistics matter greatly
Recovery is part of disaster management too

reviews.summaryZh: 这章强调在不妨碍必要救命照护的前提下保护现场与证据，并注意 responder safety。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter emphasizes preserving the scene and evidence without interfering with necessary lifesaving care, while also maintaining responder safety.
highlightsZh: patient care 和 evidence preservation 要平衡
EMS 也会影响 scene integrity
documentation 对后续调查也重要
highlightsEn: Balance patient care with evidence preservation
EMS actions affect scene integrity
Documentation matters for later investigation as well
